content.problemStatement:
Read problems statements in Russian hereThe Head Chef is interested in studying interactions between his chefs . There are ** N ** chefs with ids 1 to N . Each chef trusts some of the other chefs . The relation of trust is one way . Also , a chef may trust chefs only with ids strictly greater than his/her id .A chef with id = i , trusts the chefs with next ni id's.  


The Head Chef wants to know given a chef B and a set of chefs S,  how many lines of trust exist between each element of S and B . A line of trust between chefs A and B is a sequence of chefs a1 ... ak starting at A ( a1 = A )and finishing at B  (Ak = B) such that Ai trusts A(i+1)  for all i ( 1 to k-1) . Two lines of trust are different if they have a different chef at the some position in the line . 




Since the answer may be quite large , output it modulo 1000000007 .
constraints: 1 <= Each element of set S < B
1 <= i + ni (for i = 1 to N) <= N
content.inputFormat: First line contains two space-separated integers N and B, denoting the number of chefs and the target chef. The next N lines contain an integer ni, denoting the number of chefs trusted by the chef with id = i. The next line contains a single integer Q, denoting the number of queries. The next Q lines contain elements of set S.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print the number of lines of trust between each element of S and B, modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The lines of trust between 1 and 3 are 1, 3 and 1, 2, 3. There is one line of trust between 2 and 3, which is 2, 3.
The lines of trust between 1 and 4 are 1, 2, 4; 1, 3, 4; and 1, 4. The lines of trust between 2 and 4 are 2, 3, 4 and 2, 4.
The lines of trust between 1 and 5 are 1, 2, 5; 1, 3, 5; 1, 4, 5; 1, 5; and 1, 2, 3, 5. The lines of trust between 2 and 5 are 2, 3, 5; 2, 4, 5; 2, 5; and 2, 3, 4, 5.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming and graph theory. We first construct a graph where each chef is a node, and there is a directed edge from chef A to chef B if A trusts B. Then, we use dynamic programming to calculate the number of paths from each chef to the target chef B. We use modular arithmetic to avoid overflow.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^3) per test case, O(T * N^3) total. Precomputation is done once for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) — the dominant structure is the adjacency matrix of the graph.
